questions.question: A ship sails $18\text{ km}$ from $P$ on a bearing of $060°$ to $Q$, then $24\text{ km}$ from $Q$ on a bearing of $150°$ to $R$. Find the direct distance $PR$.
questions.explanation:
The angle between the bearings is $90°$. Use Pythagoras: $\sqrt{18^2 + 24^2} = \sqrt{900} = 30$.

**Common mistake:** A student might add the distances directly ($18 + 24 = 42$) or fail to identify that the path forms a right-angled triangle.

questions.question: From the top of a $100\text{ m}$ tower, the angles of depression of two points $A$ and $B$ on the same side are $22°$ and $38°$ respectively. Calculate the distance between $A$ and $B$.
questions.explanation:
Distance to $A = 100 / \tan 22° \approx 247.5\text{ m}$. Distance to $B = 100 / \tan 38° \approx 128.0\text{ m}$. Difference $= 247.5 - 128.0 = 119.5\text{ m}$.

**Common mistake:** A student might calculate the distances correctly but add them together instead of finding the difference between points on the same side.

questions.question: A map scale is $1 : 50000$. A lake covers an actual area of $12\text{ km}^2$. Calculate the area of the lake on the map in $\text{cm}^2$.
questions.explanation:
Scale: $1\text{ cm} = 0.5\text{ km}$. Area scale: $1\text{ cm}^2 = 0.25\text{ km}^2$. Map area $= 12 / 0.25 = 48\text{ cm}^2$.

**Common mistake:** A student might divide the area by $50000$ only once or misplace the decimal point during the km to cm conversion.

questions.question: An aircraft is observed from a control tower at an altitude of $3\text{ km}$ and a ground distance of $10\text{ km}$ on a bearing of $025°$. Calculate the direct distance (3D) from the tower to the aircraft.
questions.explanation:
This is 3D Pythagoras using the ground distance and altitude: $\sqrt{10}^2 + 3^2 = \sqrt{100} + 9 = \sqrt{109} \approx 10.44\text{ km}$.

**Common mistake:** The student might try to incorporate the bearing ($25°$) into the Pythagoras calculation unnecessarily.

questions.question: A ship sails 7 km on a bearing of 306° and then 11 km on a bearing of 070°. Calculate the direct distance from the start to the destination.
questions.explanation:
Use the Cosine Rule: $PX^2 = 7^2 + 11^2 - 2(7)(11)\cos(56°)$. This gives $PX = \sqrt{83.88} \approx 9.16$ km.

**Common mistake:** Incorrectly calculating the interior angle of the triangle from the bearings.
